challenges.title: Properly Formatted Rule
challenges.description: Write a single CSS rule targeting `body` with three declarations: set `margin` to `0`, `padding` to `0`, and `font-family` to `Arial, sans-serif`. Each declaration should be on its own line. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'body' in css.lower(), 'Should target the body element'
assert 'margin' in css.lower(), 'Should set margin property'
assert 'padding' in css.lower(), 'Should set padding property'
assert 'font-family' in css.lower(), 'Should set font-family property'
assert 'arial' in css.lower(), 'Should include Arial in font-family'
assert 'sans-serif' in css.lower(), 'Should include sans-serif as fallback'
assert css.count(';') >= 3, 'Should have at least 3 semicolons for 3 declarations'
hints: Place each declaration on its own line inside the curly braces
End every declaration with a semicolon: property: value;

challenges.title: Descendant and Child Selectors
challenges.description: Write two CSS rules: (1) use a descendant selector to target all `a` elements inside a `nav` and set `color` to `white`, (2) use a child combinator (`>`) to target `li` elements that are direct children of `ul` and set `list-style` to `none`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'nav' in css.lower(), 'Should include nav in selector'
assert 'nav' in css.lower() and 'a' in css.lower(), 'Should target a elements inside nav'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should set color property'
assert 'white' in css.lower(), 'Should set color to white'
assert '>' in css, 'Should use the child combinator (>)'
assert 'ul' in css.lower(), 'Should include ul in child selector'
assert 'li' in css.lower(), 'Should include li in child selector'
assert 'list-style' in css.lower(), 'Should set list-style property'
assert 'none' in css.lower(), 'Should set list-style to none'
hints: A descendant selector uses a space: `nav a { }` targets all a inside nav
A child combinator uses >: `ul > li { }` targets only direct children
